questions.question: Which tag is used to create a clickable link to another page?
questions.explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag creates hyperlinks. The href attribute specifies the destination URL.

questions.question: Which tag is used to display an image on a web page?
questions.explanation: The tag embeds an image. It requires a src attribute for the image path and an alt attribute for accessibility.

questions.question: Which HTML element creates a numbered list?
questions.explanation: The <ol> (ordered list) tag creates a numbered list. Each item inside uses the <li> tag.

questions.question: What is the correct order of elements for a basic blog post structure?

questions.question: The <a> tag uses the ___ attribute to specify the destination URL.
questions.explanation: The href (hypertext reference) attribute on the <a> tag specifies the URL the link points to.

questions.question: The tag uses the ___ attribute to provide alternative text for screen readers.
questions.explanation: The alt attribute provides a text description of the image for accessibility and is displayed if the image fails to load.
